text.title: Overview and core principle
text.summary: The orchestrator coordinates phase-specific subagents by handing each one a path to a versioned prompt file plus a small parameter block, instead of re-typing instructions inline.

rule.statement: The orchestrator MUST NOT inline long instructions into the `Agent` tool's `prompt` argument. It dispatches the subagent with a short message that points at a file under `prompts/` and supplies a small key-value parameter block.
rule.rationale: Behaviour drifts when the same instructions are paraphrased every run. Versioned prompt files make each phase a deterministic capability and let prompts be reviewed, diffed in git, and improved over time.

rule.statement: Each subagent prompt file MUST be self-contained — it briefs the subagent like a colleague who has not seen the parent conversation.
rule.rationale: Subagents spawned via the `Agent` tool do not inherit the parent's context window. A prompt that assumes shared state silently degrades into vague instructions.

section.title: Role split
section.p: The orchestrator does three things only: decides which phase runs next, decides what can run in parallel, and confirms sensitive actions (push, deploy, destructive ops) with the user. Everything else lives in subagents.

list.item: Orchestrator: composition, parallelism decisions, user confirmations, quota gating.
Subagents: domain work behind a single prompt file each.
Reference docs: cross-cutting knowledge (git conventions, screenshot strategy, delivery channel auth).
Playbooks: per-delivery-surface specializations (faion-net-be, faion-net-fe, knowledge, storybook, mediamanager-fe, ETL).

section.title: Tracker shape (faion-network)
section.p: The pattern uses the project's `.aidocs/` SDD lifecycle as the source of truth. Each "ticket" is a feature folder whose status is its location in the lifecycle:

list.item: `.aidocs/<project>/done/<feature-id>/` — merged locally, awaiting push or already pushed.

section.p: Each feature folder contains the SDD artifact set: `spec.md`, `plan.md` (merged `## Design` + `## Execution Plan` per plan-md-structure), conditional `user-flows.md` (user-facing) and `ui-ux-design.md` (UI), atomic `TASK_*.md` files, `readiness.md` (gate before move to done/), and a `clarifications.md` produced by the CLARIFY phase. The orchestrator never hard-codes a vendor tracker.
section.reference: Repo-wide layout including the SDD lifecycle folders.

rule.statement: User-facing strings (clarification questions, delivery captions, AskUserQuestion payloads) use Ukrainian. Code, commit messages, prompt files, reference docs, and CHANGELOG entries use English.
rule.rationale: Mixed-language code is hard to grep; mixed-language conversation is impolite. The split is consistent across runs because it is encoded in the prompt files.
